title: ranunculus ficaria subsp. nudicaulis
intro: Ranunculus ficaria subsp. nudicaulis, also known as lesser celandine, is a perennial plant species within the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) characterized by its bright yellow flowers and heart-shaped leaves. Historically, lesser celandine has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it a natural remedy for wound healing and reducing pain and inflammation. The plant's health benefits include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve heart health, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Lesser celandine is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and it is also used in herbal remedies and supplements to promote overall well-being and vitality.
